subject: Re: goldnet on warptools
body:
Hi Jesse,

The fs_motion_and_ctf program is intended only to estimate/correct motion
and estimate CTF parameters. In the expected workflow, 2D masks are
generated by BoxNet (in your case using a specific set of weights for
masking out fiducials, in general, using weights for removing high-contrast
contamination). Masked regions are replaced with noise matching local image
statistics prior to certain image processing tasks, one of which is
tomogram reconstruction.

There are separate programs for working with BoxNet but these are not yet
well documented and don't come with a pre-trained model, please remember
you are using a pre-release piece of software :-)

Please note that weights for earlier versions of BoxNet are not compatible
with Warp 2.0/WarpTools (https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/32)

Cheers,

Alister


On Thu, 1 Aug 2024 at 11:10, Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> I was under the impression that WARP removed the fiducials from the
> aligned 2D tilts, with the logic that this would avoid issues in weird back
> projection shadows.  I checked the averages folder though from windows WARP
> and saw that fiducials are still there, suggesting fiducials are removed
> during tomogram reconstruction.  So I checked whether WarpTools had any
> flags in ts_reconstruct rather than fs_motion_and_ctf and still don't see
> any flags for fiducials.
>
> Since dealing with fiducials is apparently done at the 3D level rather
> than on the 2D images I therefore don't see any way around this, so I'll
> leave the fiducials in for now.
>
> Jesse
>
> On Tuesday, July 30, 2024 at 9:53:40 AM UTC+2 Jesse Hansen wrote:
>
>> hi all,
>>
>> Is it possible to use goldnet to remove fiducials in warptools?  I don't
>> see any options for flags in fs_motion_and_ctf.
>>
>> thanks
>>
>> Jesse
>>
